operating_state: Caterpillar achieved 2024 sales and revenues of $64.8 billion, declining 3% from prior year driven by lower equipment sales volumes across Construction Industries and Resource Industries, partially offset by Energy & Transportation growth. Operating profit increased 1% to $13.1 billion with margin improving to 20.2% from 19.3%, driven by favorable price realization of $1.2 billion offsetting volume declines. Order backlog increased to $30.0 billion with Energy & Transportation growth driven by data center demand for AI and cloud computing applications.
cost_structure: Cost of goods sold decreased improving gross margin performance through favorable price realization and manufacturing cost improvements, particularly lower freight costs. Operating expenses increased with higher strategic investments in services growth, autonomy, and electrification initiatives. Restructuring costs increased primarily from non-U.S. entity divestitures reaching approximately $400 million. Financial Products interest expense increased reflecting higher average borrowing rates.
material_events: The company executed aggressive capital returns with $7.7 billion in share repurchases during 2024 compared to $5.0 billion in 2023, reducing outstanding shares to approximately 478 million. Board approved an additional $20.0 billion share repurchase authorization with $20.1 billion remaining under current authorizations. Dividends totaled $2.65 billion with quarterly dividend increased to $1.41 per share, representing 8.4% growth. The company settled IRS tax disputes for years 2007-2016 without penalties, resulting in final assessment of $490 million plus interest.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation created $556 million in losses reflecting dollar strength against major trading currencies. The company faces continued supply chain disruptions and inflationary pressures on transportation, components and raw materials. China excavator demand above 10-tons remains at relatively low levels while European economic conditions show persistent weakness. Construction spend in North America benefits from Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act funding through multi-year projects.
forward_outlook: Management expects 2025 sales and revenues to be slightly lower than 2024 due to reduced sales volume and unfavorable price realization, with Construction Industries and Resource Industries declines partially offset by Energy & Transportation growth. Services revenues are expected to grow across all three primary segments. The company anticipates restructuring costs of $150-200 million, capital expenditures of approximately $2.5 billion, and effective tax rate of 23.0% excluding discrete items.
uncertainty_profile: Ongoing tax examinations for years 2017-2019 with IRS and international jurisdictions create potential for additional assessments, with $1.3 billion in unrecognized tax benefits. Pension obligations of $12.2 billion remain sensitive to discount rate movements and demographic assumption changes. The company faces heightened cybersecurity threats requiring continuous investment in protective systems. Credit loss provisions may increase if economic conditions deteriorate, with Cat Financial maintaining elevated provisions reflecting normalized delinquency trends.
